title: 4v2 and 5v2 Build Out Diamond Rondo
summary: an excellent rondo to work on creating and using supporting angles, and penetrating forward.  4v2 with one defender fixed at a halfway line, progress to add a 5th offender centrally
pitchSize: 20 by 10 yard rectangle, with cones splitting to two 10x10 squares
instructionsHtml:
One defender must stay on the halfway line but can move side to side.  Other defender can defend in the half where the players are.
Object is to penetrate back and forth between the two 10 by 10 boxes.  Can add point per transition for offense (and point if defense recovers and dribbles out and stops the ball)
Rotate two defenders every 2 mins
progressionsHtml:
0) Add point for each transition from one half to the other (for offensive four)
1) Add point for defenders if they can dribble out of the box and stop the ball
2) Add 5th offender centrally to work on supporting angles and committing defenders
3) Reduce playing size for more difficult angles/pace

diagram.alt: 4v2 and 5v2 Build Out Diamond Rondo diagram
